purpose: Define source-safe preview validation for tenant/project rule versioning so operational rules can evolve by project without hard-coding, silent overwrites, broken historical records or real production writes.
versioningRules: Every project-level operational rule must be versioned by tenantId, projectId, ruleSetType and ruleSetVersion.
Do not overwrite an active rule silently; create a new version.
Rules with historical impact must keep previousRuleSetId and previousRuleSetVersion.
Breaking changes require migrationPlanId and rollbackPlanId before future activation.
Integration gate changes cannot activate real providers from preview.
Currency, country and payment rule changes require explicit human review.
HR mapping and questionnaire routing changes require preview validation before activation.
Sensitive data policy changes require sensitive data gate review.
Academia content policy changes must notify impacted roles in future UI, but preview only records intended impact.
Rule versioning must never hard-code a single project as global platform logic.
claudePrototypeImpact: Project settings UI should show version/draft/review/active-future states, not silently overwrite rules.
Admin should see when a rule change affects visits, questionnaire routing, payments, notifications, CRM, ranking or Academia.
UI must not say a provider/integration is active just because a rule version exists.
Show migration/rollback required for breaking changes.
Academia must explain project rule versioning, impact review and rollback.

newLessons: When a rule change is breaking
How to review impact before activation
Why provider gates remain off until explicitly authorized
interactiveChecklists: Before changing payment or currency rules

hardStops: Do not activate production rules from preview.
Do not overwrite active rules silently.
Do not enable Make, Gemini, email, WhatsApp, Storage, Firestore or HR writes from this preview.
Do not change historical records to match new rules.
Do not use one project rule as global SaaS default without explicit tenant/platform review.
Do not store secrets or sensitive payloads inside rule versions.
